title: Camissonia sceptrostigma
taxonomy: Camissonia sceptrostigma is a species of flowering plant in the family Onagraceae, commonly known as the evening primrose family. This plant is a member of the genus Camissonia, which is a group of flowering plants native to the deserts and dry regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The genus Camissonia is further classified within the tribe Onagreae, which includes about 14 genera of flowering plants. Camissonia sceptrostigma is a drought-tolerant, annual or perennial plant that typically grows in sandy or gravelly soils. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the evening primrose family.
uses: Camissonia sceptrostigma, also known as desert star or starflower, is a species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and gastrointestinal issues.
